subject: english
chapter_title: Be Smart, Be Safe (Be Smart, Be Safe)

main_sections.title: Be Smart, Be Safe
main_sections.prose_summary: The chapter is a road safety guide written as a friendly letter from a traffic police officer named Bharat, addressed to children. The letter acknowledges that roads can be busy and dangerous, and promises police support to keep children safe. It then presents six numbered rules for pedestrian safety. Rule 1 (Always Use the Zebra Crossing): look right, left, again right before crossing and cross only when safe. Rule 2 (Follow the Traffic Lights): wait for the pedestrian green signal before crossing and walk calmly, never run. Rule 3 (Walk on the Footpaths): footpaths are for pedestrians, stay away from moving vehicles. Rule 4 (Hold an Adult's Hand): always hold an adult's hand near or while crossing the road. Rule 5 (Use Reflective Stickers in the Dark): stick reflective stickers on clothes in the evening so drivers can see you. Rule 6 (Be Alert): avoid playing, using headphones, or using mobile phones near traffic, as distractions can prevent you from hearing traffic sounds. The text concludes with a 'New Words' box introducing: pedestrian, footpath, reflective, distract. The tone is warm, direct, and instructional, with each rule accompanied by an illustration.

tasks_verbatim.task_text:
A. Answer the following.
1. What should you do before crossing a road?
2. Why should you not play on the footpath?
3. Why should you hold an adult's hand while crossing the road?

question_bank.stem: What should you do before crossing a road?
Why should you not play on the footpath?
Why should you hold an adult's hand while crossing the road?

tasks_verbatim.task_text:
B. True or False.
1. You should always walk on the footpath.
2. It is safe to run while crossing the road.
3. You should use the zebra crossing to cross the road.
4. Wearing dark clothes in the evening makes you more visible.
5. It is safe to play near the road.
6. You should wear headphones while walking on the road.

question_bank.stem: You should always walk on the footpath.
It is safe to run while crossing the road.
You should use the zebra crossing to cross the road.
Wearing dark clothes in the evening makes you more visible.
It is safe to play near the road.
You should wear headphones while walking on the road.

tasks_verbatim.task_text:
C. Encircle the correct option.
1. What should you do before crossing a road?
   a) Run quickly  b) Stop, look right, then left, again right  c) Close your eyes
2. Where should you walk near the road?
   a) On the footpath  b) On the road  c) In the middle of the vehicles
3. Why should you avoid using headphones on the road?
   a) It looks bad  b) It makes you run faster  c) It can distract you from traffic sounds

question_bank.stem: What should you do before crossing a road?
options: Stop, look right, then left, again right

question_bank.stem: Where should you walk near the road?
options: In the middle of the vehicles

question_bank.stem: Why should you avoid using headphones on the road?
options: It can distract you from traffic sounds

tasks_verbatim.task_text:
D. Identify the following symbols and write their meanings.
[Help box: School ahead, No honking, No littering, Ladies restroom, Gents restroom, Right turn, Handicapped, No left turn, Drive slow, Danger ahead, U turn]
[11 road/safety symbols shown — ladies restroom, gents restroom, handicapped, U turn, no left turn, right turn, drive slow, school ahead, no honking, no littering, danger ahead]

visual_stimulus.brief_description: Eleven standard Indian road/public signs: ladies restroom, gents restroom, handicapped, U-turn, no left turn, right turn, drive slow, school ahead, no honking, no littering, danger ahead — each with a blank box for students to write the meaning.

tasks_verbatim.task_text:
A. Classify the following as "C" (Countable) and "U" (Uncountable).
• Bananas  • Lemonade  • Biscuits  • Flour  • Curd  • Chocolates  • Tomatoes  • Tomato ketchup  • Sandwiches  • Potatoes  • Mashed potatoes  • Coffee  • Coffee jars  • Tables
[Grammar box: Countable nouns can be counted (e.g. apples, buses, chairs). Uncountable nouns cannot be counted individually and usually have no plural form (e.g. water, rice, air).]

question_bank.stem: Bananas — Countable (C) or Uncountable (U)?
Lemonade — Countable (C) or Uncountable (U)?
Biscuits — Countable (C) or Uncountable (U)?
Flour — Countable (C) or Uncountable (U)?
Curd — Countable (C) or Uncountable (U)?
Chocolates — Countable (C) or Uncountable (U)?
Tomatoes — Countable (C) or Uncountable (U)?
Tomato ketchup — Countable (C) or Uncountable (U)?
Sandwiches — Countable (C) or Uncountable (U)?
Potatoes — Countable (C) or Uncountable (U)?
Mashed potatoes — Countable (C) or Uncountable (U)?
Coffee — Countable (C) or Uncountable (U)?
Coffee jars — Countable (C) or Uncountable (U)?
Tables — Countable (C) or Uncountable (U)?

tasks_verbatim.task_text:
B. Now, classify the following into countable and uncountable nouns.
Traffic, Pollution, Helmet, Buses, Cars, Speed, Pedestrian, Bicycle, Safety, Transportation, Fuel

question_bank.stem: Classify into countable and uncountable: Traffic, Pollution, Helmet, Buses, Cars, Speed, Pedestrian, Bicycle, Safety, Transportation, Fuel

tasks_verbatim.task_text:
C. Read the recipe below. Underline the countable nouns and circle the uncountable nouns.
Recipe: Yummy banana milkshake
Step 1: Peel two bananas and put them in a blender.
Step 2: Pour one glass of milk into the blender.
Step 3: Add sugar and a little honey for sweetness.
Step 4: Blend everything until smooth.
Step 5: Add some ice cubes and enjoy your banana milkshake!

A. Classify the following activities as "safe" and "unsafe".
• Looking right and left before crossing
• Playing on the road
• Crossing at a zebra crossing
• Walking on the footpath
• Listening to loud music on the road

question_bank.stem: Looking right and left before crossing — Safe or Unsafe?
Playing on the road — Safe or Unsafe?
Crossing at a zebra crossing — Safe or Unsafe?
Walking on the footpath — Safe or Unsafe?
Listening to loud music on the road — Safe or Unsafe?

tasks_verbatim.task_text: B. Spot what's wrong with the picture. Look at the following picture below and spot six errors.

visual_stimulus.brief_description: Detailed road scene showing multiple road-safety errors: traffic light on green with pedestrian crossing, child walking on road without footpath, person using headphones near traffic, incorrect zebra crossing markings, tree growing on road, and a cat walking on the road.

D. Write a letter to the traffic police of Bharat to thank them and promise to follow all the traffic rules and stay safe.
[Starter provided: Dear Traffic Police Officer, Thank you for keeping us safe on the roads. You work so hard to make sure everyone follows the rules and no one gets hurt. I see how you stand in the sun, rain, and cold to help us. I have read your letter carefully and understood that .......... Jai Hind! Name: ______ Grade: ______]
